blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: What do we actually know about Price. Any of us. Has anyone ever met him?

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: I haven't. I've spoken to people who work for him. That's as close as I've gotten.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: How would Marcus even get a direct number for someone like that.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: Marcus could get anything. You know that. He knew people who knew people and he was good enough with people that doors opened.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: Do you think Price did it.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: I told Davenport the same thing I'll tell you. It doesn't fit. Price doesn't work that way.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: People like that have people who do things for them.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: Not like this. A hit from someone like Price looks like a car accident. Or a robbery. Or just someone disappearing. It doesn't look like — this.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: So then what does it look like.
Nobody answered. Because the answer was the same one they'd been circling since Sunday morning, and it was the one none of them wanted to land on.
